titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Jeanette Hyland Papers
titlestmt.author: Josh Panos and Martha Lund Smalley

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday August 10, 2021 at 22:00

did.unittitle: Jeanette Hyland papers
did.abstract: This collection documents the life and work of Jeanette Hyland, an Australian nurse and public health official who worked in various positions as a Christian missionary for International Nepal Fellowship (INF) and United Mission to Nepal (UMN) between 1969 and 1995. Her work and ministry were primarily focused on the eradication of leprosy in Nepal. Over the course of her career, Hyland served as a nurse, health education officer, and director of national leprosy control programs. She also made various scholarly contributions to the fields of public health and social anthropology. In the later stages of her career, Hyland's research in health education and leprosy in Nepal received international recognition. She also published four books, the most recent of which is a memoir of her own life and ministry.

chronitemset.event: Born in Hobart, Tasmania, Australia
Attended Hobart High School in Hobart, Tasmania
General nursing training at Royal Hobart Hospital in Hobart, Tasmania
Midwifery training part I at General Hospital in Rochford, Essex, UK
Midwifery training part II at Churchill Hospital in Headington, Oxford, UK
Served as Nurse Educator at Royal Hobart Hospital in Hobart, Tasmania
Attended Melbourne Bible Institute in Lilydale, Victoria; received Diploma in Theology
Attended College of Nursing, Australia (CNA); received Diploma in Nursing
Served as Nurse Educator at Shining Hospital in Pokhara, Nepal
Served again as Nurse Educator at Royal Hobart Hospital in Hobart, Tasmania
Attended College of Nursing, Australia (CNA); received Diploma in Community Health Nursing
Received non-medical supervisor training (leprosy) in Karigiri, India
Served as Training, Planning, and Health Education Officer for the National Leprosy Control Programme of International Nepal Fellowship (INF) in Pokhara, Nepal
Attended the School of Public Health at the University of California, Berkeley; received Master of Public Health
Served as Health Services Secretary for United Mission to Nepal (UMN) in Kathmandu, Nepal
Returned to Australia on holiday to consider professional future
Worked as Lecturer in Advanced Nursing Studies at Tasmanian State Institute of Technology in Hobart, Tasmania
Worked as Maternity Ward Nurse at Calvary Hospital in Hobart, Tasmania
Attended the University of Tasmania, Australia; received Doctor of Philosophy in Public Health
Served as Director (part-time) of the INF Nepal Leprosy Control Project in Pokhara, Nepal

Worked as Post-Doctoral Researcher in Nepal (part-time), studying social anthropology and health education in relation to leprosy with Dr. Ulla-Britt Engelbrektsson of the University of Gothenburg, Sweden

Worked in various research and administrative positions, mostly at the University of Tasmania; wrote three books (Maids, Masters, and Magistrates, The Bhaktini of Bojha, and Village Nepal and the Diaries of Gwen Coventry 1959-1979)
Retired, pursued personal interests and wrote a memoir, Suddenly Free…

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection documents Jeanette Hyland's activities as a missionary, nurse educator, and public health administrator in Nepal between 1969 and 1995, with a focus on her work in leprosy prevention and treatment, and the work of the International Nepal Fellowship (INF) and United Mission to Nepal (UMN) in these areas. The Hyland papers also contain materials pertaining to her personal life and her late-career research activities in Australia between 1996 and 2002. See the records of the International Nepal Fellowship (RG 214), United Mission to Nepal (RG 212), and the papers of Gwen Coventry (RG 241) for related documentation.

Series I, Correspondence, consists primarily of Jeanette Hyland's personal letters to family and friends, as well as letters related to her work and official circulars (usually addressed "Dear Friends") that she wrote over the course of her career.
Series II, Writings, contains two long-form research publications, one of which is a PhD dissertation, that Jeanette Hyland wrote toward the end of her career.

Series III, Documentation of Activities, contains Jeanette Hyland's professional reports, summaries, educational curricula, and other organizational literature. Almost all of these documents were written or co-written by Hyland herself. The names and order of each sub-series are organized by the various positions that Hyland held over the course of her career. With only a few exceptions, the order and contents of this series have been preserved exactly as Jeannette Hyland arranged them.
Series IV, Collected Material, consists of a few published books and articles written by colleagues that Jeanette Hyland collected over the course of her career.

Series V, Audio-Visual Material, contains one unlabeled photograph and a few blank photographic postcards that Jeanette Hyland collected as well as audiocassette recordings of sermons from INF conferences in 1983 and 1994 and a recording of a UMN organizational meeting in 1986.

Jeanette Hyland also published four books: Maids, Masters, and Magistrates: Twenty Women of the Convict Ship New Grove: Maid Servants in Van Diemen's Island (2007), Gwen Coventry in Nepal: Bhaktini of Bojha (2010), Village Nepal and the Diaries of Gwen Coventry, 1959-1979 (2011), and Suddenly Free…: Memoir of a Tasmanian Nurse (2018). The books published in 2007, 2011 and 2018 are cataloged in the Yale library system as restricted access titles and can be viewed in the Yale Divinity special collections reading room upon request.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jeanette Hyland Papers (RG 318), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, 1961-2002
Personal letters
To/from sister and brother-in-law, to/from friends

To/from sister and brother-in-law, to/from friends

To/from sister and brother-in-law
To/from sister and brother-in-law, to/from friends
To/from sister and brother-in-law, to/from friends
To/from sister and brother-in-law, to/from friends
To/from sister and brother-in-law
To/from sister and brother-in-law
To/from sister and brother-in-law
Letters related to work

Commendation for first ministry post in Nepal, UC Berkeley application materials, update about work to sister and brother-in-law, miscellaneous newspaper clippings, miscellaneous notes and letters about position, funding, and sponsorship

Letters in response to Jeanette's INF resignation, prospective UMN employee letters, letters about INF retirement fund, conference itineraries, letters pertaining to new UMN position, "What has happened" summary of recent work/travels, miscellaneous notes and letters about position, funding, and sponsorship, INF LCP acceptance letter, pre-furlough evaluation, retirement fund letters, "A Missionary in Nepal" address
Bible study notes, documents pertaining to leprosy control project calling and goals, personal fundraising letters and ministry updates, letters considering furlough/retirement
Personal journal from Tasmanian holiday 1987-1988 (includes reflections on professional future)

Letters about possibly going to do a PhD, financial documents, house blueprints, documents pertaining to exchange with elders on the role of women in Murray Street Chapel, INF memos, letters to/from former co-workers
Letters to/from other leprosy organizations and hospitals, letters to/from former co-workers, letters about research funding, letters outlining travel plans
Internal INF memos, letters to/from other leprosy organizations and hospitals, letters to/from former co-workers, letters about research funding, letters outlining travel plans

Internal INF memos, letters to/from other leprosy organizations and hospitals, letters to/from former co-workers, letters about research funding, letters outlining travel plans, letters about published letter in the Leprosy Review, letters about retirement
Letters about post-retirement research, letters outlining financial transactions
Letters about post-retirement research, letters to/from former co-workers
Circular letters
Regular ministry updates
Regular ministry updates, annual post-retirement updates
Writings, 1993-1995
Doctoral Thesis, University of Tasmania [Part I]
Doctoral Thesis, University of Tasmania [Part II]
The Role of the Community in Integrating People Affected by Leprosy: A Paper on the Social Aspects of Leprosy, Presented to the Leprosy Mission Internation Medical Reference Panel
Documentation of Activities, 1969-2002
1969-1972 – Nurse Educator, Shining Hospital – INF Pokhara, Nepal
Health education curricula, nurse training documents, various health reports
1973-1974 – Diploma in Community Health Nursing, Melbourne
Various diploma assignments
1975-1979 – Training, Planning, and Health Education Officer, National Leprosy Control Program – INF Pokhara, Nepal
Nurse education documents, papers written for INF written during degree at Berkeley
1979 – Master of Public Health, University of California, Berkeley
Various MPH assignments
1980-1982 – Training, Planning, and Health Education Officer, National Leprosy Control Program – INF Pokhara, Nepal
UMN commission on institutional implications in health care
Various standing orders, memos, draft protocols, and internal reports for INF, five year plan
National epidemiological survey on leprosy
Meeting minutes, training workshop report
Internal reports for INF, case studies
Training curriculum for leprosy supervisors
Training curriculum for leprosy supervisors
Paramedical worker curriculum
Paramedical worker curriculum
Paramedical worker curriculum
1983-1987 – Health Services Secretary, United Mission to Nepal
Leprosy training course documents, workshop documents, conference paper
Speech notes, internal UMN planning documents
1987-1994 – Doctoral Preparation Field Research, University of Tasmania
Application form for field research in Nepal, published article and accompanying notes
"Singleness" essay, health education documents
Curriculum vitae, field research papers
Nepali interview transcripts
Nepali interview transcripts
Nepali interview transcripts
Nepali interview transcripts
Materials from "training for trainers" workshop
Nepal visit report, notes on Nepalese words used for leprosy, field research papers
1993-1995 – Director, National Leprosy Control Project – INF Pokhara, Nepal
LCP annual report, program evaluation documents
LCP annual report, supervision checklist, budget summary
LCP internal report, workshop opening remarks
Materials for/from LCP donors meeting, internal LCP report
History of leprosy control in Nepal document
Health position handover protocol materials
LCP budget summary, annual report
1996-2002 – Research and writing in Tasmania
Research materials, updated curriculum vitae, health education documents
Collected Material, 1982-2012
Robert J. Cardinalli – At the Feet of Lord Vishnu: An Ethnographic Study of Leprosy in Nepal
Carl W. Friedericks – "Over Thirty," Richard C. Friedericks – "The Development, Production and Distribution of Health Education Materials in Leprosy"
J.P. Baral – "Status of Leprosy Elimination Programme and Plan of Action for Leprosy Elimination in Nepal"
Ulla-Britt Engelbrektsson – Challenged Lives: A Medical Anthropological Study of Leprosy in Nepal
Audio-Visual Material
Undated photograph of friend, miscellaneous blank/undated postcards
Recordings from International Nepal Fellowship Conference (five audiocassette tapes)
Recording of Tansen station meeting with Ingaborg and Friedericks (one audiocassette tape)
Recordings from International Nepal Fellowship Conference (three audiocassette tapes)
